causal_chains.description: Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex
Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load)
Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 3 (33.7% – 36.2% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.058s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 713.0 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex (confidence: 52%)
    💡 Tip: Try initiating your braking earlier and with more initial pressure to scrub speed before the turn-in point.
    → Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load) (confidence: 42%)
    💡 Tip: The excess entry speed is overloading the front tyres. Carry less speed in to maintain grip.
zones.llm_feedback: You're carrying excess speed into the apex, which is likely due to insufficient braking - try to increase your brake pressure by a few percent to match the expert's level. As you're currently 713 rpm below the expert's reconstruction, you have room to add more throttle earlier in the corner exit, aiming to utilize the full throttle range. To improve your line, focus on reducing your steering wheel angle by around 6-7 degrees to minimize understeer and high lateral load. By making these adjustments, you'll be able to optimize your corner exit speed and overall lap time.

summary_llm: The largest area for improvement lies in optimizing throttle application and braking technique, as evidenced by the significant RPM deficits across all zones, with a maximum deficit of 1009.8 rpm in Zone 4. To address this, focus on applying the throttle earlier in the corner exit phases, such as in Zone 1, where late throttle application results in an 834.7 rpm deficit. Despite these challenges, it's encouraging to see that the driver is able to maintain a high overall severity rating of 100% across all zones, indicating a strong foundation to build upon. By refining their technique, the driver can aim to shave off the estimated total time loss of ~1.305s and achieve a faster lap time.
